evidenceTrail.title: Zero audience thread
citedDetails: zero audience feels like silence
founder said marketing with zero audience feels like silence
evidenceTrail.excerpt: A founder said marketing with zero audience feels like silence. The comments that worked were genuine answers to real questions, no pitching, while direct product pitches were ignored or downvoted.

evidenceTrail.title: Monday product note
citedDetails: I do not need more templates, I need to know what is safe to say today.
five posts at once, but the useful insight is the customer quote: "I do not need more template
weekly review should choose one post, not fill a calendar
evidenceTrail.excerpt: We keep trying to write five posts at once, but the useful insight is the customer quote: "I do not need more templates, I need to know what is safe to say today." The weekly review should choose one post, not fill a ca…

angles.whyThisAngle: The source is conversational, pain is already visible, and a helpful reply can outperform another top-level post.
angles.suggestedComment: The part I would double down on is the non-pitch answer: zero audience feels like silence (C1). A useful reply could add one concrete example, then ask what the person has already tried. No product mention.
suggestedEditPrompts: Add one first-person example that proves zero audience feels like silence (C1).
Remove any sentence that could be copied into another founder's post unchanged.
If you cannot add one concrete detail from this week, choose skip.
angles.recommendationRationale: Best bet for an early-stage founder: timely because the source material is recent enough to act on now, credible because it cites C1, N1, and relevant because it preserves concrete pain/details instead of manufacturing a generic post.

citedDetails: LinkedIn audiences are reacting badly to generic openers, smooth transitions, and symmetrical AI p
founder/operator evidence is performing better than polished filler
evidenceTrail.excerpt: LinkedIn audiences are reacting badly to generic openers, smooth transitions, and symmetrical AI post structures. Specific founder/operator evidence is performing better than polished filler.

angles.whyThisAngle: It compresses the source material into one debatable lesson with citations.
angles.draftPost:
A helpful reply can beat a louder post.

The signal is in the details: zero audience feels like silence (C1); founder said marketing with zero audience feels like silence (C1).

an early-stage founder should only post this if they can add one concrete example from this week's work. Without that, it becomes filler.

angles.draftPost:
I do not need more templates, I need to know what is safe to say today. (N1) is the part worth posting, not the polished lesson around it.

The useful point: We keep trying to write five posts at once, but the useful insight is the customer quote: "I do not need more templates, I need to know what is safe to say today." The weekly revi….

For an early-stage founder, this is a credibility play because the post can show the messy decision behind the work instead of pretending the insight arrived fully formed.

If I posted this, I would keep the receipts visible: [N1], [C1], [L1].
